content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "Erasing Zeroes". Refer to LeetCode description: https://codeforces.com/problemset/problem/1303/A
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Then T test cases follow. The first line of each test case contains an integer N, the number of elements in the array. The second line of each test case contains N space-separated integers.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the maximum number of zeroes that can be erased.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Count the number of zeroes in the array and return the minimum between the count of zeroes and the count of non-zero elements plus one.

code.Python3:
t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    n = int(input())
    count = 0
    zero = 0
    for x in map(int, input().split()):
        if x == 0: zero += 1
        else: count += 1
    print(min(zero, count + 1))
